text: Seek wealth, not money or status. Wealth is having assets that earn while you sleep. Money is how we transfer time and wealth. Status is your place in the social hierarchy.
You're not going to get rich renting out your time. You must own equity — a piece of a business — to gain your financial freedom.
Arm yourself with specific knowledge, accountability, and leverage.
Specific knowledge is knowledge you cannot be trained for. If society can train you, it can train someone else and replace you.
Code and media are permissionless leverage. They're the leverage behind the newly rich. You can create software and media that works for you while you sleep.
Learn to sell. Learn to build. If you can do both, you will be unstoppable.
Escape competition through authenticity. When you're competing with people, it's because you're copying them.
Play long-term games with long-term people. All returns in life, whether in wealth, relationships, or knowledge, come from compound interest.
Desire is a contract you make with yourself to be unhappy until you get what you want.
Happiness is a state where nothing is missing.
Peace is happiness at rest, and happiness is peace in motion.
A fit body, a calm mind, a house full of love. These things cannot be bought — they must be earned.
The modern mind is overstimulated and the modern body is understimulated and overfed. Meditation, exercise, and fasting restore an ancient balance.
A busy mind accelerates the perceived passage of time. Buy more time by cultivating peace of mind.
The peace that we seek is not peace of mind, it's peace from mind.
Don't take yourself so seriously. You're just a monkey with a plan.
If you can't decide, the answer is no.
It's the mark of a charlatan to try to explain simple things in complex ways. It's the mark of a genius to explain complicated things in simple ways.
A contrarian isn't one who always objects — that's a conformist of a different sort. A contrarian reasons independently, from the ground up, and resists pressure to conform.
Cynicism is easy. Mimicry is easy. Optimistic contrarians are the rarest breed.

Mentors won't make you rich. Doctors won't make you healthy. Nutritionists won't make you slim. Teachers won't make you smart. Gurus won't make you calm. Trainers won't make you fit. Ultimately, you have to take responsibility. Save yourself.

What to study and how to study it are more important than where to study it and for how long. The best teachers are on the Internet. The best books are on the Internet. The best peers are on the Internet. It's the desire to learn that's scarce.
We each have a contrarian belief society rejects. The more our own identity and local tribe reject it, the more real it likely is.
If you're so smart, how come you aren't happy? How come you haven't figured that out? That's my challenge to all the people who think they're so smart and so capable.
Society will pay you for creating the things it wants. But society doesn't yet know how to create those things — because if it did, they wouldn't need you.
Retirement isn't doing nothing. Retirement is never sacrificing today for something you don't actually want to do.
Real wealth is complete ownership of your time. If your calendar is filled by other people, you're not rich yet.
Execution is a codifiable skill. It can be trained, replicated, automated. Specific knowledge cannot.
Every unfulfilled desire is a contract you signed with unhappiness. Keep only one at a time.
If a book could teach what you do, it isn't specific knowledge. Specific knowledge looks like intuition — accumulated residue of unstructured learning.
Don't fix the instance. Rebuild the system that produced the problem.
When evaluating someone, watch what they did under pressure, not what they say when things are fine.
If a job can be reduced to a standard operating procedure, it will eventually be automated or templated. Choose work that requires judgment.
Whose permission does this require? Where's the permissionless path?
If your views on a topic map perfectly onto a particular group, you're mimicking, not thinking. The fewer identity labels you carry, the better.
Ask: if I could only do one thing, which one makes me lose track of time? That's specific knowledge speaking.
Real winning is not competing.
You can systematically manufacture luck. There are four levels: blind chance, action, preparation, and character that makes luck come to you.
Draft left to right. Edit right to left. Cut until only the essence survives.
When you're anxious, list your current desires. The anxiety lives in their conflict, not in your circumstances.
